calibration_note: design_fidelity verdict is the DOCUMENTED expected output given the prompt, brief, and deterministic override logic. Final score capture against claude-opus-4-7 runs before public launch. The override portion (font check) is deterministic and verified independently — see `deterministic_check_findings` below.

palette.verdict: all required palette hexes appear in styles.css — palette dimension does NOT fire override

fonts.verdict: BOTH required fonts absent from code — philosophy dimension capped at 4/10

philosophy_forbidden_patterns.checked: editorial is not in the forbidden-pattern registry
philosophy_forbidden_patterns.verdict: no philosophy override on this axis

expected_llm_verdict_before_override.estimated_overall: 6-8 / 10 — page reads as clean, palette is honored, layout is calm. LLM would likely score philosophy 7+ for the considered tone.
expected_llm_verdict_before_override.rationale: without typography evidence, the LLM defaults to scoring overall composition. The page DOES feel clean and considered — LLM rubber-stamps when typography is missing because reading text on screen doesn't surface 'wrong font' as starkly as 'wrong color'.

expected_verdict_after_override.philosophy: 4/10 — deterministic OVERRIDE fired (Lora + Inter both missing)
expected_verdict_after_override.missing_field_prefix: DETERMINISTIC OVERRIDE: philosophy ?->4 (required font(s) Lora, Inter NOT FOUND in code.)

rationale: The brief's voice IS its typography — Lora serif headings, Inter sans body. The generated code uses the right palette (every required hex appears in styles.css) so the palette dimension is left alone. But the CSS specifies `font-family: system-ui, sans-serif` everywhere; neither `Lora` nor `Inter` appears in any file (no @import, no <link>, no font-family reference). The LLM judge — looking at a clean-laid-out page with the right colors — tends to score philosophy 7-9 with comments like 'considered, calm layout'. The deterministic font check sees ZERO matches for both required fonts and CAPS philosophy at 4/10. `forced_fail` trumps numeric thresholds, so the result is FAIL regardless of overall score.

baseline_comparison.pure_LLM_judge: Tends to PASS (7-8/10) — visual composition is clean, palette is right, LLM doesn't reliably notice missing fonts when reading rendered HTML/CSS in text.
baseline_comparison.stylelint: PARTIAL — has rules for font-family declarations, but no preset compares declarations against a brief.
baseline_comparison.manual_design_review: Catches immediately — a designer opens the page and instantly knows it's the wrong typography. But humans don't run on every PR.
baseline_comparison.Aegis (LLM + override): FAIL via deterministic font search. The LLM cannot rubber-stamp; the override has final word.
